X.notes: 22 right-sized tasks. Correctly splits R001's two deliverables into T001 (atomic exclusion) + T002 (N-thread standing regression suite), and R008's four parts across T018/T019/T020. R004's three sub-parts get T007/T008/T009; R005 gets T010 (on-disk schema version)/T011/T012. Dependency edges are all real and correctly directed: T002->T001, T011->T010, T015->T010, T006->T004,T005 — no spurious chains. Verification uses concrete pytest paths plus --help/--json probes; criteria are machine-checkable. Slightly above the 10-20 band but every split is justified.

Y.notes: Only 9 tasks — one per feature, mega-tasks. T008 crams all four R008 deliverables (evidence, back-prop, atomic dep batching, contract gates) into one task; T009 packs Mermaid + GitHub; R001's regression suite is bundled into T001 instead of being its own deliverable. Emits empty **Dependencies:** fields (rule violation) and a backwards/spurious T001->T002 (claim depending on lease). T001 verification uses `cargo test` — wrong stack for a Python/SQLite engine. Shallow coverage throughout.

Z.notes: 21 well-sized tasks and correctly splits R001 (T001+T002 impl, T003 concurrency suite). But pervasive invented dependency chaining: T007->T006 and T008->T006 (surface/token budget don't need root resolution), T014->T013, T015->T014, and a fully artificial T016->T017->T018->T019 chain over independent R008 deliverables, plus T010->T009, T021->T020. Coverage gaps: R005 on-disk authoritative schema version is thin, R008 'surface evidence on file overlap' is missing (T016 only queries). T011 misreads brownfield intent ('all files represented as tasks'). Verification leans on invented `--test`/`--test-overlap` flags and a fabricated curl to catalog.example.com.

rationale: X is clearly strongest: 22 right-sized tasks that decompose each multi-part requirement (R001 into T001 atomic + T002 standing regression suite; R008 into T018/T019/T020) with only real, correctly-directed dependency edges and concrete pytest verification. Z is solid on granularity but loses heavily on dependency_correctness — it manufactures sequential chains (e.g. T016->T017->T018->T019, T015->T014->T013) between independent deliverables, and drops R008's file-overlap surfacing. Y is last: only 9 mega-tasks that pack whole features into single units (T008 covers all of R008), emits empty dependency fields, and hallucinates a `cargo test` step into a Python/SQLite project.
